questions.q: In the Puranas, Kalki is described as which kind of avatar of Vishnu?
options: The future avatar yet to appear
A purely allegorical figure with no descent
questions.explanation: Kalki is prophesied in the Puranas as the tenth and future avatar of Vishnu, yet to appear.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, in which age (yuga) does Kalki appear?
options: At the dawn of Satya Yuga
At the end of Kali Yuga
In the middle of Treta Yuga
questions.explanation: The Puranas prophesy that Kalki appears at the end of Kali Yuga, the present age of darkness.

questions.q: Which animal does Kalki ride according to Puranic prophecy?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Kalki riding a white horse when he appears at the close of the present age.

questions.q: Which weapon does Kalki wield in the Puranic prophecy?
questions.explanation: Kalki is prophesied to wield a blazing sword to destroy the wicked at the end of Kali Yuga.

questions.q: Kalki is regarded as an incarnation (avatar) of which deity?
questions.explanation: Kalki is counted among the avatars of Vishnu, the preserver who descends to restore dharma.

questions.q: In the classical list of the Dashavatara, Kalki holds which position?
questions.explanation: Kalki is the tenth avatar in the classical sequence of Vishnu's Dashavatara.

questions.q: What is the chief purpose attributed to Kalki's coming in the Puranas?
options: To destroy the wicked and end the age of darkness
To rule as an emperor forever
questions.explanation: The Puranas say Kalki comes to destroy the wicked and bring the present age of darkness to a close.

questions.q: After Kalki's appearance, the Puranas say a new golden age will dawn. Which age is this?
questions.explanation: The Puranas teach that Kalki ushers in the renewal of dharma and the beginning of a new Satya Yuga.

questions.q: The color of the horse traditionally associated with Kalki is which of the following?
questions.explanation: Kalki is traditionally depicted mounted on a white horse in the Puranic prophecy.

questions.q: Kalki's mission is most closely tied to the restoration of which principle?
questions.explanation: Kalki is prophesied to restore dharma, the cosmic and moral order, at the end of Kali Yuga.

questions.q: In the cosmology of the four yugas, where does Kalki's appearance fall within a single chaturyuga cycle?
options: At the very beginning of the cycle
At the close of the final age, Kali Yuga
questions.explanation: Kalki appears at the close of Kali Yuga, the last of the four declining ages in a chaturyuga.

questions.q: Which Purana is traditionally devoted to the prophecy and deeds of the avatar Kalki?
questions.explanation: The Kalki Purana is the text traditionally dedicated to the prophecy of the future avatar Kalki.

questions.q: Within the Dashavatara, which avatar immediately precedes Kalki in the commonly cited sequence?
questions.explanation: In the common classical list, Buddha precedes Kalki, who is the tenth and final avatar.

questions.q: Kalki is grouped with which other avatars of Vishnu under a single collective name?
questions.explanation: Kalki is the tenth of the Dashavatara, the ten principal descents of Vishnu.

questions.q: Which Purana, among the great Mahapuranas, contains a well-known account foretelling the coming of Kalki?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana includes an account foretelling the appearance of Kalki at the end of Kali Yuga.

questions.q: The blazing sword wielded by Kalki primarily symbolizes which act?
questions.explanation: Kalki's blazing sword represents the destruction of the wicked and the cleansing of the age of darkness.

questions.q: Kalki's role as a future avatar reflects Vishnu's broader function in the Trimurti as which of the following?
questions.explanation: As an avatar of Vishnu, Kalki expresses Vishnu's role as preserver who descends to uphold dharma.

questions.q: The name of the yuga that Kalki is said to bring to an end, Kali Yuga, is described in the Puranas as which kind of age?
options: The age of the first creation
The age of the great deluge
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Kali Yuga as the present, darkest age, which Kalki's coming brings to a close.

questions.q: Which statement best reflects the Puranic teaching about when Kalki appears among the avatars of Vishnu?
options: He is yet to appear in the future
questions.explanation: Unlike the earlier avatars, Kalki is the avatar yet to come, prophesied for the future.

questions.q: The Puranas associate Kalki's advent with the renewal of which great cosmic principle that diminishes through the yugas?
questions.explanation: Dharma steadily diminishes through the yugas, and Kalki's advent is said to renew it fully.

questions.q: Kalki shares with the other avatars of Vishnu the common purpose described in the Puranas as which of these?
options: Restoring dharma when cosmic order falters
Teaching the art of warfare to kings
Building the cities of the gods
questions.explanation: Each avatar of Vishnu, including Kalki, descends to restore dharma when cosmic order falters.

questions.q: Which emblem is most fittingly associated with Kalki, given his prophesied mount?
questions.explanation: Because Kalki is prophesied to ride a white horse, the horse is his most fitting emblem.

questions.q: In the Kalki Purana, the avatar Kalki is said to be born in which village?
questions.explanation: The Kalki Purana places Kalki's birth in the village of Shambhala at the end of Kali Yuga.

questions.q: According to the Kalki Purana, who is the father of the avatar Kalki?
questions.explanation: The Kalki Purana names Vishnuyasha, a learned brahmin of Shambhala, as the father of Kalki.

questions.q: In the Kalki Purana, the white horse of Kalki bears which name?
questions.explanation: The Kalki Purana gives the name Devadatta to the swift white horse that Kalki rides.

questions.q: Within the Vishnu Purana's framing, the appearance of Kalki marks the transition into which renewed age?
questions.explanation: The Vishnu Purana frames Kalki's coming as inaugurating a renewed Satya (Krita) Yuga of righteousness.

questions.q: In the Kalki Purana, from which sage does Kalki receive martial training and divine weapons?
questions.explanation: The Kalki Purana relates that Parashurama, the immortal sixth avatar, instructs Kalki in arms.

questions.q: The Kalki Purana names the principal adversaries Kalki confronts. They are chiefly associated with what?
options: The building of the bridge to Lanka
The personified evils of the Kali age
questions.explanation: In the Kalki Purana, Kalki battles forces personifying the corruption and adharma of the Kali age.

questions.q: Kalki's descent at the close of one great cycle and the dawn of a new one reflects which broader Puranic teaching about the cosmos?
options: The cosmos passes through repeating cycles of ages and renewal
The cosmos is created once and never renewed
Only Kali Yuga ever truly exists
The yugas grow progressively more righteous
questions.explanation: Kalki's advent illustrates the Puranic teaching that the cosmos passes through repeating cycles of ages and renewal.

questions.q: Which description best fits how the Puranas portray Kalki, in contrast to avatars such as Rama and Krishna?
options: A deity who never takes human form
A prophesied future avatar rather than one whose earthly deeds are already recounted
An avatar of Shiva rather than Vishnu
A goddess emerging from the churning of the ocean
questions.explanation: Unlike Rama and Krishna, whose deeds the texts already recount, Kalki is portrayed as the prophesied future avatar yet to come.
